
arXiv RSS signals are almost entirely NLP/LLM-focused and yield no qualifying additions. Grok social signals surface three high-confidence non-NLP transformer candidates not present in the README: an equivariant graph transformer for atomic potentials (Chemistry/Materials), a single-cell genomics foundation model (Biology), and a protein transformer for conditional generation (Protein/Molecular). A universal time-series tokenizer model is a borderline addition given the already well-covered time-series section.

## Suggestions

- [EGTP: Equivariant Graph Transformer Potentials](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.05388) — Equivariant graph transformer for learning interatomic potentials, combining SE(3)-equivariance with transformer attention for accurate molecular dynamics and materials property prediction.
  Section: Chemistry & Materials Science | Criteria: Applies transformer architecture (graph transformer with self-attention) to a non-NLP domain (materials science/chemistry), Addresses a domain-specific task with measurable performance (interatomic potential energy surface modeling), Iterative training loop grounded in domain data (atomic configurations and energies/forces), Empirical outcome demonstrating advantage (benchmark results on molecular dynamics datasets)

- [scGPT-pro](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.05879) — Efficient self-supervised transformer foundation model for single-cell genomics, scaling to large cell atlases for cell-type annotation, gene perturbation prediction, and multi-omics integration.
  Section: Biology & Genomics | Criteria: Applies transformer architecture to a non-NLP domain (single-cell genomics), Addresses domain-specific tasks with measurable performance (cell-type classification, perturbation prediction), Iterative pretraining on large-scale single-cell sequencing data, Empirical outcome demonstrating advantage over prior single-cell models on benchmark tasks

- [ProteinNPT](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.00149) — Combines transformers with normalizing flows for property-conditioned protein sequence generation, enabling controllable design of proteins with target fitness or stability profiles.
  Section: Protein & Molecular Structure | Criteria: Applies transformer architecture to a non-NLP domain (protein sequence design), Addresses a domain-specific task with measurable performance (conditional protein generation, fitness prediction), Iterative training on protein sequence-fitness datasets, Empirical outcome demonstrating advantage on protein engineering benchmarks

- [UniTime: A Language-Empowered Unified Model for Cross-Domain Time Series Forecasting](https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.02862) — Universal transformer model with domain-aware time tokenizers enabling zero-shot and few-shot cross-domain time-series forecasting across heterogeneous datasets.
  Section: Time-Series & Signals | Criteria: Applies transformer architecture to a non-NLP domain (time-series forecasting), Addresses a domain-specific task with measurable performance (cross-domain zero-shot forecasting benchmarks), Iterative pretraining on diverse time-series corpora with domain tokenization, Empirical outcome demonstrating advantage over domain-specific forecasting models
